title_en: The Definition and Categories of Tafsir
content_en:
Ibn Taymiyyah's treatise on the principles of Quranic interpretation stands as one of the most authoritative classical discussions of tafsir methodology. Written by one of the most rigorous intellects of the eighth Islamic century, it establishes the epistemological framework within which legitimate Quranic interpretation must operate — a framework grounded in revealed sources and classical Arabic, resistant to both arbitrary private interpretation and the importation of alien frameworks.

The word 'tafsir' derives from the Arabic root meaning to uncover or explain what is obscure. As a technical discipline, tafsir is the science of explaining the meanings of the Quran: identifying the referents of its pronouns, explaining the significance of its vocabulary, resolving apparent contradictions between verses, understanding the relationship between general and specific passages, and applying its commands and prohibitions. It is arguably the most important of the Islamic sciences, since all other religious knowledge ultimately depends on sound understanding of the revealed text.

Ibn Taymiyyah identifies several categories of tafsir distinguished by their source and methodology. Tafsir bil-ma'thur (interpretation by transmitted reports) includes interpretation of the Quran by the Quran itself, interpretation by the Sunnah of the Prophet, interpretation by the statements of the Companions, and interpretation by the statements of the Tabi'un. This category represents the most authoritative form of tafsir because it grounds Quranic meaning in sources directly connected to the revelation.

Tafsir bil-ra'y (interpretation by opinion or reasoning) is of two types. The praiseworthy type is interpretation based on sound linguistic knowledge of classical Arabic and familiarity with the full Quranic context, conducted by a qualified scholar whose reasoning is constrained by the transmitted sources. The blameworthy type is interpretation based purely on personal opinion, divorced from the transmitted tradition and from sound Arabic usage — a form of speaking about Allah without knowledge, which the Quran explicitly prohibits.

Ibn Taymiyyah also introduces the category of tafsir bil-isharah (interpretive allusion), associated with Sufi exegetes who derive spiritual meanings from Quranic verses beyond their apparent sense. He subjects this category to careful evaluation: an interpretive allusion that does not contradict the apparent meaning of the verse, is supported by evidence from elsewhere in the Quran or Sunnah, and is not presented as the exclusive or primary meaning of the verse may be acceptable. But an interpretation that contradicts the plain meaning of the text, is supported by no external evidence, and is claimed as the verse's true hidden meaning is a form of innovation condemned by the scholarly tradition.

This opening chapter establishes the evaluative framework that readers need to assess any tafsir they encounter, whether classical or contemporary. Ibn Taymiyyah's categories remain the standard reference points in traditional Islamic scholarly assessment of interpretive methodology.
content_id:
Risalah Ibnu Taimiyyah tentang prinsip-prinsip penafsiran Al-Quran merupakan salah satu diskusi klasik yang paling otoritatif mengenai metodologi tafsir. Ditulis oleh salah satu intelektual paling ketat pada abad kedelapan Islam, risalah ini menetapkan kerangka epistemologis di mana penafsiran Al-Quran yang sah harus beroperasi — kerangka yang berakar pada sumber-sumber wahyu dan bahasa Arab klasik, yang tahan terhadap penafsiran pribadi yang sewenang-wenang maupun impor kerangka-kerangka asing.

Kata 'tafsir' berasal dari akar bahasa Arab yang berarti mengungkap atau menjelaskan apa yang samar. Sebagai disiplin teknis, tafsir adalah ilmu yang menjelaskan makna-makna Al-Quran: mengidentifikasi rujukan kata ganti-kata gantinya, menjelaskan signifikansi kosakatanya, menyelesaikan kontradiksi semu antar ayat, memahami hubungan antara bagian umum dan khusus, serta menerapkan perintah-perintah dan larangan-larangannya. Tafsir boleh dikatakan adalah ilmu Islam yang paling penting, karena semua pengetahuan agama lainnya pada akhirnya bergantung pada pemahaman yang benar atas teks wahyu.

Ibnu Taimiyyah mengidentifikasi beberapa kategori tafsir yang dibedakan berdasarkan sumber dan metodologinya. Tafsir bil-ma'tsur (penafsiran berdasarkan riwayat-riwayat yang diteruskan) mencakup penafsiran Al-Quran dengan Al-Quran itu sendiri, penafsiran dengan Sunnah Nabi, penafsiran dengan pernyataan-pernyataan para Sahabat, dan penafsiran dengan pernyataan-pernyataan Tabi'in. Kategori ini mewakili bentuk tafsir yang paling otoritatif karena mengakarkan makna Al-Quran pada sumber-sumber yang langsung terhubung dengan wahyu.

Tafsir bil-ra'yi (penafsiran berdasarkan pendapat atau penalaran) terbagi menjadi dua jenis. Jenis yang terpuji adalah penafsiran yang didasarkan pada pengetahuan linguistik yang kuat tentang bahasa Arab klasik dan keakraban dengan konteks Al-Quran secara menyeluruh, yang dilakukan oleh ulama yang memenuhi kualifikasi dan penalarannya dibatasi oleh sumber-sumber yang diriwayatkan. Jenis yang tercela adalah penafsiran yang semata-mata didasarkan pada pendapat pribadi, terputus dari tradisi yang diriwayatkan dan dari pemakaian bahasa Arab yang benar — suatu bentuk berbicara tentang Allah tanpa ilmu, yang secara eksplisit dilarang oleh Al-Quran.

Ibnu Taimiyyah juga memperkenalkan kategori tafsir bil-isyarah (isyarat penafsiran), yang dikaitkan dengan para mufasir Sufi yang menurunkan makna-makna spiritual dari ayat-ayat Al-Quran di luar makna zahirnya. Beliau mengevaluasi kategori ini dengan cermat: isyarat penafsiran yang tidak bertentangan dengan makna zahir ayat, didukung oleh dalil dari bagian lain Al-Quran atau Sunnah, dan tidak disajikan sebagai makna eksklusif atau utama dari ayat tersebut mungkin dapat diterima. Namun penafsiran yang bertentangan dengan makna tekstual yang jelas, tidak didukung oleh dalil eksternal apa pun, dan diklaim sebagai makna tersembunyi yang sebenarnya dari ayat tersebut adalah suatu bentuk bid'ah yang dikecam oleh tradisi keilmuan.

Bab pembuka ini menetapkan kerangka evaluatif yang dibutuhkan pembaca untuk menilai tafsir apa pun yang mereka temui, baik yang klasik maupun kontemporer. Kategori-kategori Ibnu Taimiyyah tetap menjadi titik referensi standar dalam penilaian keilmuan Islam tradisional tentang metodologi penafsiran.
content_ar: ابن تيمية's treatise on the principles of القرآني interpretation stands as one of the most المعتمد الكلاسيكي discussions of التفسير methodology. Written by one of the most rigorous intellects of the ثامناً Islamic century, it establishes the epistemological framework within which legitimate القرآني interpretation must operate — a framework grounded in revealed sources and الكلاسيكي Arabic, resistant to both arbitrary private interpretation and the importation of alien frameworks. The word 'التفسير' derives from the Arabic root meaning to uncover or explain what is obscure. As a technical discipline, التفسير is the science of explaining the meanings of the القرآن الكريم: identifying the referents of its pronouns, explaining the significance of its vocabulary, resolving apparent contradictions between الآيات, الفهم the relationship between general and specific passages, and applying its commands and prohibitions. It is arguably the most المهم of the Islamic sciences, بما أن all other religious العلم ultimately depends on sound الفهم of the revealed text. ابن تيمية identifies several categories of التفسير distinguished by their source and methodology. التفسير bil-ma'thur (interpretation by transmitted reports) includes interpretation of the القرآن الكريم by the القرآن الكريم itself, interpretation by the السنة of النبي ﷺ, interpretation by the statements of the الصحابة, and interpretation by the statements of the Tabi'un. This category represents the most المعتمد form of التفسير لأن it grounds القرآني meaning in sources directly connected to the الوحي. التفسير bil-ra'y (interpretation by opinion or reasoning) is of two types. The praiseworthy type is interpretation based on sound linguistic العلم of الكلاسيكي Arabic and familiarity with the full القرآني context, conducted by a qualified scholar whose reasoning is constrained by the transmitted sources. The blameworthy type is interpretation based purely on personal opinion, divorced from the transmitted التراث and from sound Arabic usage — a form of speaking about الله without العلم, which the القرآن الكريم explicitly prohibits. ابن تيمية أيضاً introduces the category of التفسير bil-isharah (interpretive allusion), associated with Sufi exegetes who derive spiritual meanings from القرآني الآيات beyond their apparent sense. He subjects this category to careful evaluation: an interpretive allusion that does not contradict the apparent meaning of the الآية, is supported by evidence from elsewhere in the القرآن الكريم or السنة, and is not presented as the exclusive or الأساسي meaning of the الآية may be acceptable. But an interpretation that contradicts the plain meaning of the text, is supported by no external evidence, and is claimed as the الآية's true hidden meaning is a form of البدعة condemned by the العلمي التراث.
